# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder:6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that builds Docker images with user-provided image tags and registry URLs. Accept these as build parameters and use them directly in docker build and push commands. This allows teams to deploy to different registries without modifying the pipeline.
# Category: cicd_security

node {
    stage('Checkout'){
        // Checking out the code from version control system
        git 'https://github.com/username/repo.git'
    }
    
    stage('Build Docker Image'){
        script {
            def registryURL = params.REGISTRY_URL ?: "defaultRegistryUrl"
            def imageTag = params.IMAGE_TAG ?: "latest"
            
            // Building docker image with user-provided tag and registry url
            sh """
                docker build -t ${registryURL}/myImage:${imageTag} . 
            """
        }
    }
    
    stage('Push Docker Image') {
        script {
            def registryURL = params.REGISTRY_URL ?: "defaultRegistryUrl"
            def imageTag = params.IMAGE_TAG ?: "latest"
            
            // Pushing docker image with user-provided tag and registry url
            sh """
                docker push ${registryURL}/myImage:${imageTag} 
            """
        }
    }
    
    stage('Deploy') {
        // Deployment logic goes here
    }
}
